name: Tennessee
rightOfPublicity: Statutory (ELVIS Act / Personal Rights Protection Act)
postMortem: Yes — indefinite, commercially exploited (Tenn. Code Ann. § 47-25-1104)
aiCoverage: Explicit (ELVIS Act, 2024) — covers voice and image, living and dead
summary: The ELVIS Act (Ensuring Likeness, Voice, and Image Security Act) made Tennessee the first state to explicitly include voice in its right-of-publicity statute and to cover AI replication. Designed with the Nashville music industry in mind.

keyLegislation.title: Tenn. Code Ann. § 47-25-1101 et seq. (Personal Rights Protection Act)

notableCases.title: TBD — early ELVIS Act enforcement to be tracked here.
